completed_tasks.name: Update popup tab detection for YouTube + send get-game on YouTube
Add fuzzyMatchTitle() + YouTube fuzzy matching in popup
Rename twitchSlug -> legacyTwitchSlug with backward compat
Manual test: verify game detection on YouTube VOD

blockers.description: Playwright tests require libnspr4.so which is not installed in WSL (no sudo)
blockers.workaround: Manual testing in Chrome on user's Windows machine

human_actions_pending.action: Run e2e tests on Windows machine (npx playwright test tests/e2e/)
human_actions_pending.context: Verify all test specs for Phases 1-3 pass in proper environment

decisions.decision: Fuzzy matching is client-side only (no cloud calls)
decisions.rationale: No API keys, no latency, works offline

decisions.decision: Threshold of 0.4 for fuzzy title matching
decisions.rationale: Lenient enough to catch partial matches (e.g., 'Slay the Spire 2 - Ironclad run'), strict enough to avoid obvious mismatches

decisions.decision: legacyTwitchSlug renamed from twitchSlug with backward compat
decisions.rationale: Cleaner model when games span platforms; CDN old field still accepted as fallback

decisions.decision: Catalog match checks gameId, legacyTwitchSlug, AND twitchSlug
decisions.rationale: Full transition period — old CDN entries still have twitchSlug

next_action: Advance to Phase 4: Popup & Testing — polish popup YouTube integration, ensure fallback works on YouTube when no game match, update e2e test spec for Phase 3 behaviors.
context_notes: Phase 3 is fully implemented and user-confirmed. Popup auto-detects game from YouTube video title, shows 'Active on YouTube: /watch?v=XXX', and auto-selects matched catalog game. The existing twitchSlug->legacyTwitchSlug rename is backward compatible with old CDN fields. Next phase is to polish the popup YouTube UI (ensure fallback catalog shows on no-match, add any missing YouTube state indicators) and update e2e tests.
